question: What must always be included in the email subject line when sending any case-related email?
reason: All case emails must include the Incident ID in the subject line so the case can be tracked.

question: Which email address must always be added to the CC list when sending a case-related email?
reason: ltalink@ncs.com.sg must always be CC'd in all case-related emails.

question: When a user provides the information that was requested, what should the agent reply with?
optionB: An acknowledgement that the details have been received and will be reviewed
reason: The agent should acknowledge receipt and inform the user that their details will be reviewed.

question: When escalating a case to the support team, what must the agent do in Infinity?
optionC: Assign the case to the appropriate support group
reason: The case must be assigned to the correct support group in Infinity when escalating.

question: When sending an escalation email to the support team, what content must be included in the body?
optionB: A summary of the issue and the troubleshooting steps already taken
reason: The escalation email must include the issue description and all troubleshooting steps already performed.

question: What customer details must be included when sending an escalation email to the support team?
optionB: Name, contact number, company name, and vehicle number
reason: The escalation email requires the user's name, contact number, company name, and vehicle number.

question: After escalating a case to the support team, what should the agent request from them?
optionC: To update the Service Desk on the status
reason: The agent must ask the support team to update the Service Desk on the case status.

question: A user emails in about an issue and the agent needs more information to investigate. What should the agent ask for?
optionB: Vehicle number, screen capture of the error, and details of the transaction being performed
optionC: The user's NRIC and company registration number
reason: These three items — vehicle number, error screenshot, and transaction details — are the standard information requested.

question: When should an agent send the "Reply to User asking for more information" email template?
optionA: After the case has been resolved
optionB: When the agent does not have enough details to investigate the issue
optionC: When the case is being closed
optionD: When the user asks to speak to a manager
reason: This template is used when the agent needs additional details before they can investigate further.

question: What should the agent tell the user will happen after they provide the requested details?
optionB: The issue will be resolved immediately
optionC: The agent will check with the relevant support team
optionD: The user will need to call in
reason: The agent must inform the user that once details are received, they will be checked with the relevant support team.

question: A user's Netrust software needs to be reinstalled. In what order should the steps begin?
optionA: Install the software first, then restart
optionB: Unplug the token first, then check for old software versions
optionC: Restart the PC first, then plug in the token
optionD: Plug in the token first, then install
reason: The correct first step is to unplug the token, then check for and uninstall old software versions.

question: When reinstalling Netrust software, which old software versions should be removed first?
optionA: Old version of Java, old version of Entrust Entelligence Security Provider, and old version of SafeNet Authentication Client
optionB: Old version of Chrome, old Java, and old Netrust drivers
optionC: Old VPN software and old browser plugins
reason: All three — old Java, old Entrust Entelligence, and old SafeNet Authentication Client — must be uninstalled before reinstalling.

question: After successfully reinstalling the Netrust software, what must the user do before trying to log in?
reason: After installation, the PC must be restarted before the user attempts to log in.

question: A user cannot see the "Apply or Renew VPC" function on LTALink. What should the agent advise?
optionA: Ask the user to reinstall their browser
optionB: Ask the user to contact LTA directly
optionC: Ask the user to approach their CorpPass Admin to assign the "HV Park Operators" role
reason: The HV Park Operators role must be assigned by the CorpPass Admin for the user to access VPC functions.

question: If a user has been assigned the "HV Park Operators" role but still cannot see the VPC function, what should the agent request?
optionB: A screenshot showing functions visible after login and a screenshot from CorpPass confirming the role was assigned
optionC: The user's NRIC and company registration
reason: These two screenshots allow the agent to verify what the user sees versus what role has been granted
